rag_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by adding or updating only the intended owner's annex content without changing component structure, features, subcomponents, or connections.
Keep contracts in valid owner scope: component type contracts belong on the type owner, while implementation-level content belongs on the implementation owner.
Do not move a port-based contract into an implementation just because an implementation target was provided; preserve valid scope and ownership rules.
generic_rules: Modify the smallest valid owning element needed for the generated content.
Preserve existing declarations and non-AGREE model text; merge with existing annex content instead of rewriting unrelated sections.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use standard AGREE annex block form when present in the fused result: annex agree {** ... **};
Avoid references that are only valid in a different owner scope, such as subcomponent paths in a type-level contract.
anti_patterns: Do not insert contract content into an owner where referenced symbols are not visible.
Do not alter architecture elements or relocate unrelated model content while fusing annex blocks.
irrelevant_topics: Temporal equation patterns and logical operator details beyond owner/scope preservation
Validator-specific scalar type repairs and Boolean-vs-numeric comparison fixes
